retry_reason: Put operation never fails; if v is NULL no put is attempted so retry is pointless.
contract_summary: This function is the seq_file stop callback for iterating over BPF programs. Its main role is to release the reference on the current bpf_prog object (v) that was acquired during the start/next callbacks by calling bpf_prog_put. If v is NULL, it calls __bpf_prog_seq_show for final output (unrelated to refcount). The caller (seq_file core) does not need to check return value. After this call, the pointer v must not be used; the caller must have previously obtained the reference appropriately.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a reference on a bpf_prog object when it is the current element in seq_file iteration; the put on non-NULL v is the key refcount operation. The NULL path calls an unrelated show function, but that is an edge case.
judge.retry_reason: Put operation never fails; if v is NULL no put is attempted so retry is pointless.

check.reason: bpf_prog_put is called on v (parameter 2) when v is not null. The access path v.aux.refcnt correctly traces from v (struct bpf_prog *) through aux to refcnt, matching the callee info. Segment counts (3) and type chain (3 types) are consistent. The operation direction 'put' aligns with the callee being a put function.
